lede: A look at May 7 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Ashlyn Krueger and Alexander Ludwig, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Indian Army
events.desc: The Indian Army and the Indian Air Force conduct surgical strikes code-named Operation SINDOOR on terrorist hideouts in Pakistan in response to the Pahalgam Attack that killed 26 people.

events.title: 2023 Tanur boat disaster
events.desc: Tanur boat disaster: At least 22 people are killed when a boat carrying tourists capsizes in Tanur, Malappuram, Kerala, India.

events.title: Kosovo War
events.desc: Kosovo War: Three Chinese citizens are killed and 20 wounded when a NATO aircraft inadvertently bombs the Chinese embassy in Belgrade, Serbia.

events.title: Space Shuttle program
events.desc: Space Shuttle program: The Space Shuttle Endeavour is launched on its first mission, STS-49.

events.title: Bright Sparklers fireworks disaster
events.desc: A fire and explosion occurs at a fireworks factory at Sungai Buloh, Malaysia, killing 26.

events.title: Battle of the Coral Sea
events.desc: World War II: During the Battle of the Coral Sea, United States Navy aircraft carrier aircraft attack and sink the Imperial Japanese Navy light aircraft carrier Shōhō; the battle marks the first time in naval history that two enemy fleets fight without visual contact between warring ships.

events.title: 1930 Salmas earthquake
events.desc: The 7.1 Mw Salmas earthquake shakes northwestern Iran and southeastern Turkey with a maximum Mercalli intensity of IX (Violent). Up to three-thousand people were killed.

events.title: Clipper
events.desc: The world's oldest surviving clipper ship, the City of Adelaide is launched by William Pile, Hay and Co. in Sunderland, England, for transporting passengers and goods between Britain and Australia.

events.title: French Revolution
events.desc: French Revolution: Robespierre introduces the Cult of the Supreme Being in the National Convention as the new state religion of the French First Republic.

events.title: Louis XIV
events.desc: Inaugural celebrations begin at Louis XIV's new Palace of Versailles.

births.name: Jan Peter Balkenende
births.line: Dutch jurist and politician, Prime Minister of the Netherlands

births.name: Sidney Altman
births.line: Canadian-American biologist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate (died 2022)

deaths.text: Steve Albini, American musician, record producer, audio engineer, and music journalist
Big George, English songwriter, producer, and radio host
C. F. W. Walther, German-American religious leader and theologian

observances.text: Christian feast day: Harriet Starr Cannon (Episcopal Church (USA))
